reasoning: While programmatic text metrics show high content retention (0.979 recall), there is a significant discrepancy with the visual fidelity score (0.47). The primary purpose of the document—a geographic map visualization—has been lost in translation, with the map itself missing and the logo fragmented into quadrants. Because the spatial relationship of the data is the core of this document, the collapse into a vertical text list requires human review to ensure the information remains meaningful and accessible.

concerns.analysis: The source document is a geographic status report centered around a map of North Carolina. The layout uses spatial positioning and color-coding to convey information (status of parcel projects by county). The HTML output completely collapses this visual organization into a simple vertical list of text and logo fragments. The visual flow of the original (Map -> Legend -> Metadata) is replaced by a disconnected list of assets (Logo pieces -> Legend text -> Scale text -> County name list). This des
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A central map showing 100 North Carolina counties color-coded to a legend.
concerns.output_quote: The map is entirely missing; instead, there is a wall of text containing county abbreviations at the bottom of the page.

concerns.analysis: The most meaningful image in the source PDF is the map graphic itself, which functions as a data visualization diagram. This image is completely absent from the HTML output. Additionally, the legend's color swatches (which are small graphical icons/squares) are missing from the HTML list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The colored map of North Carolina and the colored status boxes in the 'Parcel Updates' legend.
concerns.output_quote: The map is missing; the 'Parcel Updates' list contains only text bullets without the corresponding color indicators.
concerns.location: Page 1, map area and legend section

concerns.analysis: The overall visual quality is poor. The NCGICC logo, which is a single integrated graphic in the PDF, is rendered as four separate, disconnected quadrants in the HTML. The conversion feels like a raw extraction of text and image paths rather than a cohesive document representation. The county abbreviations are just a mass of unformatted text at the end of the file.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The NCGICC logo in the bottom right corner (a single integrated unit).
concerns.output_quote: The logo is split into four separate image blocks followed by text.

action_items: Assess whether the missing map graphic can be represented via complex alt-text or if the layout requires a different conversion approach.
Re-combine the fragmented logo quadrants into a single accessible image.
Ensure the county list provides sufficient context to the reader in the absence of the geographic visualization.
